hyperedges.label: Stylesheet Linking Pattern — colors_and_type.css + components.css required in every HTML file
Preview HTML Cards collectively implement the OneWELL Design System components

nodes.label: Knowledge Promotion Flow (vault → codex → forge → Jarvis)
nodes.rationale: Raw material arrives in vault/, distilled understanding in codex/, repeatable procedures in forge/, and reusable cross-project knowledge is promoted to Jarvis. This layered promotion ensures stable, well-reviewed knowledge surfaces upward.
nodes.norm_label: knowledge promotion flow (vault → codex → forge → jarvis)

nodes.label: Five Core Working Principles
nodes.rationale: 1) Use existing tokens never invent. 2) Brand marks are sacred. 3) Type pairing is fixed. 4) Cyan is the brand yes — one gradient CTA per view. 5) One canonical home per component. These enforce consistency and prevent design drift.

nodes.label: Design System Anti-Patterns
nodes.rationale: Hardcoded hex, non-brand fonts, missing .ow-btn base class, multiple primary CTAs per view, concept colors on chrome, recolored seals, decorative emoji, bright gradients as text background, title-case headings, and generic error messages are all flagged in review.

nodes.label: WELL Voice & Tone Guidelines
nodes.rationale: Calm, factual, generous, never alarmist. Sentence case for headings, title case for certification levels and proper nouns. Error format: what happened + next step. No urgency-baiting, no cheery error language.

nodes.label: Brand Mark Protection Rules
nodes.rationale: Seals and badges must never be recolored, redrawn, cropped, or have effects applied. They must be used verbatim. Seals must render ≥64px on long edge; below that use badge variant. This preserves IWBI brand integrity.

nodes.label: Accessibility Minimums
nodes.rationale: Non-negotiable: visible focus rings (2px cyan-300), 44×44px touch targets, ≥4.5:1 contrast for body and caption text, status chips always color+label, descriptive alt text on all seals/badges, semantic HTML elements, associated labels for inputs.

nodes.label: Atomic Design Hierarchy (Foundations→Tokens→Elements→Components→Patterns→Brand→Iconography→Guidelines)
nodes.rationale: The system follows Brad Frost's Atomic Design adapted for the WELL brand-heavy context, organizing from foundations through patterns and brand assets to ensure progressive specificity and easy onboarding.
